title: subagent: block_in_place + Handle::block_on inside sync tool dispatch can deadlock
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/subagent.rs:1340-1350 (execute_task_tool sync branch)
## Violation
```rust
let result = match Handle::try_current() {
    Ok(handle) => tokio::task::block_in_place(|| {
        handle.block_on(run_subagent(&config, app_config, &client))
    }),
    Err(_) => match tokio::runtime::Runtime::new() {
        Ok(rt) => rt.block_on(run_subagent(&config, app_config, &client)),
```

`tokio::task::block_in_place` PANICS under `current_thread` runtimes (commonly used by CLI/test harnesses). `Handle::block_on` from within a runtime worker is itself a known deadlock anti-pattern when the inner future yields back to the same single-threaded executor. The fallback `Runtime::new()` spawns a fresh runtime per tool call: leaks threads, can starve the outer I/O reactor, and bypasses cancellation.
## Mandated refactor
Make `execute_task_tool` async (return a future) and let the tool dispatcher await it. The sync wrapper should live at the CLI boundary only. Remove the `Runtime::new()` fallback — fail fast with a typed error when no runtime is in scope.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
